summary.intent: Resolve OpenShift cluster upgrade blockers preventing migration from OCP 4.18 to 4.19, specifically addressing warnings about elasticsearch-operator.v5.8.21 and cluster-logging.v6.1.9 having maximum supported OCP version capped at 4.18.
summary.outcome: Successfully removed the deprecated elasticsearch-operator (subscription, CSV, pod, and orphaned CRDs) and upgraded cluster-logging from v6.1.9 to v6.4.3, clearing both operator-related upgrade blockers. Two remaining blockers identified: cgroup v1 migration and admin acknowledgment for API removals in Kubernetes 1.32.

workflow: OLM operator upgrade paths are constrained by skipRange in ClusterServiceVersions - direct channel jumps may not work if current version falls outside the skipRange
Elasticsearch operator for OpenShift is deprecated/EOL (scheduled for catalog removal November 2025) and only supports ServiceMesh, tracing, and Kiali use cases
ClusterVersion Upgradeable condition reconciles periodically and may not immediately reflect changes after operator removal
Upgrading cluster-logging from v6.1.9 to v6.4.x requires two-step channel hop: stable-6.1 -> stable-6.3 (to get v6.3.4) -> stable-6.4 (to get v6.4.3) due to skipRange constraints
Always verify CRD instances are empty (kubectl get <crd>) before deleting CRDs left behind by uninstalled operators
ClusterLogging instances can use Vector for collection without requiring Elasticsearch as a log store

friction: OLM skipRange constraints prevented direct upgrade from cluster-logging v6.1.9 on stable-6.4 channel, requiring intermediate channel hop through stable-6.3
ClusterVersion Upgradeable condition showed stale elasticsearch-operator blocker immediately after removal (lastTransitionTime from February), requiring wait for reconciliation
open_items: Migrate cluster from cgroup v1 to v2 (required for 4.19 upgrade, triggers rolling node reboots): oc patch nodes.config.openshift.io cluster --type merge -p '{"spec":{"cgroupMode":"v2"}}'
Admin acknowledgment for Kubernetes 1.32 API removals in OCP 4.19: review KB article 7112216 and oc patch cm admin-acks in openshift-config namespace
